name: Gravity Wave Backgammon
framing: A sheet of recycled plastic, turned into a backlit backgammon board.

blocks.body: A brief set by Gravity Wave, the company that makes the recycled-plastic material: take a 1m × 1m board and increase its value.
We chose to make a backgammon board — drawn by the material's visual character, and by the craft and value of a good board: an object you would want to look at for hours.

The rules didn't allow any other materials, so we couldn't simply colour the black and white pieces. No inlays, no paint, no second material to distinguish the dark and light triangles that define a backgammon field — we had to render the playing grid using the single material alone.

Instead we found that milling the material down to 1–3mm let varying amounts of light pass through it. By alternating layer thicknesses across the board, we used light to render the entire backgammon pattern — keeping the surface completely flat and smooth.
We prototyped in 3D and CNC'd early versions from wood — iterations to confirm the form and the playing-surface dimensions before committing to the single 1m × 1m sheet.

blocks.alt: Wood prototypes, before the final CNC pass
Detail — light passing through the thinned material
